summary:
Rescue crews in Venezuela are still searching through rubble four days after twin earthquakes killed more than 1,400 people and left 50,000 unaccounted for. The government of Delcy Rodríguez is facing growing criticism over its response, as families wait for news and hope fades. The true scale of the disaster is still emerging, and officials have not said when the search might end.

The United States has reached a deal with Iran to keep vessels moving freely through the Strait of Hormuz, a US official said, though Iran has not confirmed the agreement. The development comes as peace talks are set to resume, but the lack of Iranian confirmation leaves the deal uncertain. In a separate conflict, Pakistan launched airstrikes into Afghanistan that the Taliban says killed 36 civilians. Pakistan claims it targeted 32 militants. The cross-border fighting has continued for months, and both sides are sticking to their own casualty numbers.

The world's largest recorded Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak continues in the Democratic Republic of Congo, with 1,274 confirmed cases and 360 deaths. There is some progress: the number of recoveries nearly doubled in a week, rising to 148. Doctors are learning more about this rare strain as treatment centers admit dozens of patients each day. Meanwhile, a US-Kazakhstan agreement has given a group of American investors with ties to President Trump and Commerce Secretary Lutnick access to one of the world's largest tungsten reserves. The deal, worth billions, has drawn attention because of the personal connections involved.

headlines.title: US Reaches Deal with Iran to Halt Attacks on Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: The agreement would allow vessels to move freely through the Strait of Hormuz, a US official said. Iran has not confirmed the deal, as peace talks are set to resume.

headlines.title: Venezuela Searches for 50,000 Missing After Earthquakes as Death Toll Exceeds 1,400
headlines.summary: Four days after the twin earthquakes, rescue teams continue searching for survivors amid growing criticism of the government's response. Over 1,400 dead and 50,000 unaccounted for.

headlines.title: Pakistan Strikes Kill 36 Civilians in Afghanistan, Taliban Says
headlines.summary: Taliban says airstrikes killed 36 civilians, while Pakistan claims 32 militants were targeted. The attack is the latest in a monthslong cross-border conflict.

headlines.title: Confirmed Ebola Cases in Congo Reach 1,274 with 360 Deaths
headlines.summary: The world's largest Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak continues, with recoveries doubling to 148 in a week. Doctors study the rare virus as treatment centers admit dozens daily.

headlines.title: Trump-Cut Billion-Dollar Mining Deal with Kazakhstan Benefits His Sons
headlines.summary: The deal gives a group of American investors with ties to President Trump and Commerce Secretary Lutnick access to one of the world's largest untapped tungsten reserves.
